name: hypothalamic_drive
description: Minimal hypothalamus — homeostatic drive generator. Creates intrinsic motivation signals that persist until satisfied. Real hypothalamus: integrates internal body state (blood glucose, temperature, osmolarity) to generate drive signals (hunger, thirst, arousal). Our version: 2 IB integrator neurons accumulate drive (tonic self-excitation = slowly building motivation). 1 CH burst neuron fires when drive reaches threshold (the 'urge' signal). 1 FS inhibition neuron receives satisfaction signal and kills the drive. 1 RS output provides continuous drive level. 1 LTS provides slow recovery after satisfaction (refractory period before drive builds again). The key feature: drive BUILDS over time from self-excitation, even without external input. Satisfaction kills the drive. Then it slowly rebuilds. Implements: intrinsic motivation, homeostatic regulation, drive-reduction behavior, arousal modulation.
